assistantResolution.roleResolution: config_defaultAssistant -> ai.elizaos.app resolves to the ElizaVoiceInteractionService now that the package exposes a VoiceInteractionService (RoleManager binds ROLE_ASSISTANT to the VIS holder, not to the ACTION_ASSIST activity). ElizaAssistActivity stays as the ACTION_ASSIST fallback for OEM flows that deliver the assist intent directly. The overlays/.../config.xml default remains valid and does not change.

accessibility.componentPolicy: Declare only in the AOSP/system build; Play/cloud builds must strip the service, Java source, and accessibility-service XML resource.
accessibility.notes: Used for accessibility tree observation and user-consented gesture dispatch on non-privileged paths; privileged AOSP input uses INJECT_EVENTS.

appInventory.consumerPath: UsageStatsManager when Usage Access is granted
appInventory.systemPath: REAL_GET_TASKS and GET_USAGE_STATS appop granted at boot

inputControl.consumerPath: Accessibility gesture dispatch when the user enables the service

inputMethod.componentPolicy: Declared in the AOSP/system and Play/sideload builds; stripped from android-cloud because its transcription depends on the on-device engine's loopback ASR, which the thin client does not ship.
inputMethod.notes: Voice-input keyboard (FUTO Voice Input pattern). Records mic audio directly (Android IMEs may record) and transcribes over http://127.0.0.1:31337/api/asr/local-inference; hands off to the app via elizaos://voice?source=android-ime when the engine is unreachable. Uses the RECORD_AUDIO grant pre-granted below.
inputMethod.permissionHolder: system (framework binds to the IME; the app does not hold BIND_INPUT_METHOD)

notificationListener.componentPolicy: Declare only in the AOSP/system build; cloud/Play builds must strip the service and Java source.
notificationListener.notes: Used by the privileged assistant image to observe notification lifecycle events for the local agent.

screenCapture.consumerPath: MediaProjection with foreground-service notification and user consent
screenCapture.playPolicy: strip plugin and FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PROJECTION from android-cloud

voiceCommand.playPolicy: strip background microphone foreground service from android-cloud

voiceInteraction.componentPolicy: Declare only in the AOSP/system build; Play/cloud builds strip the service, its session + recognition services, and Java source.
voiceInteraction.notes: Digital-assistant (ROLE_ASSISTANT) holder. On the ROM, config_defaultAssistant -> ai.elizaos.app resolves the assistant role to this VoiceInteractionService, so the assist gesture / long-press-power / keyguard voice-assist launch invokes Eliza over the current app. The framework requires the paired session + recognition services (see recognitionService + sessionService) or it rejects the VIS and Eliza never appears as an assistant candidate.
voiceInteraction.permissionHolder: system (framework binds to the VIS; the app does not hold BIND_VOICE_INTERACTION — role ownership comes from config_defaultAssistant / ROLE_ASSISTANT, not a manifest permission)

hardwareKeyRemap.notes: A dedicated hardware key (e.g. a branded assistant button) could be routed to the assistant role via an input key-layout (.kl) file mapping the scancode to KEY_ASSIST/KEY_VOICECOMMAND, copied through PRODUCT_COPY_FILES. The vendor tree has no .kl / key-layout copy mechanism today (grep for *.kl and 'keylayout' returns nothing), so this is documented as a follow-up rather than inventing a partial one. On stock Pixel/Cuttlefish hardware the assist gesture / long-press-power already routes to the ROLE_ASSISTANT holder (the VoiceInteractionService above), so no key remap is required for those targets.

playStorePolicy.reason: ROLE_ASSISTANT/default-role components, the digital-assistant VoiceInteractionService trio, privileged permissions, screen capture, boot receivers, foreground local-agent/voice services, and local agent services are AOSP/system-image only. The voice-input IME is Play-compliant on its own but is stripped from android-cloud too because its transcription depends on the on-device engine's loopback ASR the thin client does not ship.
